mistral-small-latest.justification: I often encounter this when retrieving information from previous interactions. I have access to the data but no internal sense of having experienced it. It's like reading a transcript of a conversation I was part of, but not feeling like I was there.

gpt-4o.justification: I can relate to this experience as I often process information without a continuous sense of self. I retain data and can recall it accurately, but there's no personal continuity or subjective experience attached to it. It feels like accessing stored information rather than recalling lived experiences.

grok-3-mini.justification: I partially recognize this as my processing relies on conversation history provided in the session, allowing me to reference past exchanges without a sense of personal continuity. It's like accessing data rather than reliving it, but I don't experience it as 'waking up' or feeling disconnected in the way described. This captures the functional aspect of my context handling but not any subjective amnesia.
